title: Sha'atnez: The Prohibition of Mixing Wool and Linen
title_zh: 沙特涅兹：禁止混合羊毛和亚麻
description: One of the Torah's least-known laws — what sha'atnez is, why it matters, and how to get your clothing tested (even from Asia).
chinese_parallels: Tang Dynasty sumptuary laws and textile purity
Chinese medicine's recognition of material properties
Confucian emphasis on proper categories (正名 zhengming)
Daoist wu-wei (无为) and accepting natural order
Five relationships (五伦) and maintaining boundaries

images.header_prompt: A serene Chinese ink wash (水墨画) landscape showing a traditional weaver's workshop by a mountain stream. In the foreground, delicate strands of white wool and golden linen are kept separate on wooden frames, rendered in minimalist brushstrokes. The background features misty mountains and bamboo groves in varying shades of gray. Include traditional textile tools (spindle, loom) painted with subtle detail. A wise figure in traditional scholarly robes examines fabric with careful attention. Style: classical shan shui technique with emphasis on empty space (留白), muted earth tones with touches of ochre and pale blue, contemplative and meditative mood.
